  error_handling:
    - "TrackgenticError carries result code, message, and exitCode. 13 error codes defined."
    - "ErrorCodes: NOT_INITIALIZED(1), ALREADY_INITIALIZED(0), TOKEN_REQUIRED(2), INVALID_TOKEN(3), DEFAULT_USER_MISSING(4), NOT_FOUND(5), ISSUE_MISSING(6), COMMENT_NOT_FOUND(7), USER_ALREADY_EXISTS(8), USER_NOT_FOUND(9), INVALID_PARAMS(10), BLOCKAGE_CYCLE(11), HIERARCHY_CONSTRAINT(12)."
    - "HIERARCHY_CONSTRAINT (exit 12) used in Phase 5 — parent status would constrain child, or parent is closed."
    - "BLOCKAGE_CYCLE (exit 11) now active — used in Phase 6 blockages when addBlockage would create a cycle."
  phase_5_hierarchy:
    - "hierarchy.ts exports pure validation functions: isStatusAfter, validateNewChild, validateParentStatusChange, validateReparent, canCloseIssue."
    - "index-manager.ts extended to track parentId and children arrays in IndexEntry."
    - "Cascade-close: when parent transitions to closed, all non-closed children are auto-closed recursively."
    - "Status progression: idea -> todo -> in-progress -> done -> closed. Child must not be ahead of parent."
    - "Done parents ARE allowed — the constraint is only on closed parents for new children."
    - "CLI --parentId flag added to create and update commands."
    - "Prettier formatting issues found and fixed in test files during quality validation."
  phase_6_blockages:
    - "dependency-manager.ts is a pure function module — all functions take DependenciesFile and return a new DependenciesFile (immutable pattern). No filesystem I/O inside pure functions; readDependencies/writeDependencies are separate async functions."
    - "Bidirectional maps (blockedBy + blocks) must always stay in sync. Every mutation function (add/resolve/delete) updates both maps. Tests verify both sides explicitly."
    - "detectCycle uses BFS walk of blockedBy graph from blockerId. Only considers active entries. Uses visited set for safety against pre-existing cycles."
    - "Batch atomicity: blockagesAdd validates ALL blockers against a projected (deep-cloned) state before writing. If any causes a cycle, the entire batch is rejected with no side effects."
    - "Auto-resolution: integrated into Tracker.update() AFTER main update logic. When issue transitions to done/closed, reads deps, filters active blocks entries, resolves each, appends system-authored blockage-resolved events to blocked issues."
    - "Impact score in list sort: readDependenciesSync (synchronous) used inside sort comparator because sort is synchronous. Lazy-loads deps once during sort."
    - "Impact score sort order: priority ASC → impact score DESC → id ASC (age). Higher impact (more issues blocked) sorts first within same priority."
    - "CLI blockages commands use --by flag for blocker IDs (comma-separated or repeated). blockages list takes a single positional issueId."
    - "Auth integration tested for blockages: add/resolve/delete are write operations (require auth in read-only/strict), list is a read operation (allowed in read-only, blocked in strict)."
    - "dependency-manager.test.ts is 19 pure function tests with 35 assertions — no filesystem needed. Empty DependenciesFile fixture used as starting point."
    - "Tracker blockages section: ~33 tests in tracker/tracker-blockages.test.ts. Covers: happy paths, batch atomicity, cycle detection, NOT_FOUND for blocked/blocker, NOT_INITIALIZED, auto-resolution for done/closed, auto-resolution no-op for no active blocks, selective auto-resolution (only active, skip resolved), impact score sort verification."
    - "Blockages auth integration section: 9 tests in tracker/tracker-blockages.test.ts covering read-only mode (TOKEN_REQUIRED for add/resolve/delete, OK for list), strict mode (TOKEN_REQUIRED for all including list), valid token (author from token in events), invalid token (INVALID_TOKEN)."
    - "CLI blockages section: 10 tests covering add (single + multiple + cycle error + NOT_FOUND), resolve, delete, list (happy path + empty arrays + NOT_FOUND)."
    - "Self-block test: detectCycle catches A blocked by A — the BFS starts at blockerId which equals blockedId, immediately returns true."
  phase_7_next_command:
    - "`next <user-name>` is a read-only suggestion command — no mutations, no events appended."
    - "Filtering logic: only idea/todo/in-progress statuses eligible (done/closed excluded). Only issues assigned to the given user. Only issues with NO active blockages (resolved blockages are OK)."
    - "Sort order: priority ASC → impact score DESC → id ASC (age). Same as list() sort but with the additional blocked-issues filter."
    - "Returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE (result code, not error) when no matching issues — exit code 0, not an error."
    - "Case-sensitive assignee matching — 'alice' != 'Alice'. This is intentional."
    - "14 unit tests in tracker/tracker-next.test.ts (256 lines): basic priority sort, blocked exclusion, impact tiebreak, NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE (empty + all blocked + wrong user), NOT_INITIALIZED error, done/closed exclusion, resolved blockages, case-sensitive matching, full ComputedIssue fields, ID tiebreak, cross-user isolation, status eligibility (idea/todo/in-progress only)."
    - "6 CLI tests in commands.test.ts: basic case, blocked exclusion, NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE, NOT_INITIALIZED error, done/closed exclusion, resolved blockages treated as unblocked."
    - "tracker.ts grew from 1329 to 1734 lines with the next() method addition."
    - "cli/commands/next.ts is 22 lines — thin wrapper that calls tracker.next() and outputs JSON."
    - "cli/runner.ts grew from 164 to 171 lines with next subcommand registration."
"}}

# Spec: `next` Command — Restrict to `todo` Status Only

## Summary

Change the `next <user-name>` subcommand to only consider issues with `todo` status, removing `idea` and `in-progress` from the eligible statuses. The `next` command is meant to recommend the best piece of work to pick up next — `idea` issues haven't been vetted yet, and `in-progress` issues are already being worked on. Only `todo` represents work that's been triaged, approved, and ready to start.

## Requirements

1. The `Tracker.next(assignee)` method must filter the open index to issues where `status === "todo"` (previously: `status in ["idea", "todo", "in-progress"]`)
2. All other behavior remains unchanged: assignee filter, blockage exclusion, priority/impact/id sorting, `ComputedIssue` return shape, `NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE` when empty
3. Existing tests that rely on the old status filter must be updated to reflect the new behavior
4. New tests should explicitly verify that `idea` and `in-progress` issues are excluded

## API/interface changes

No API changes — same method signature, same return type (`NextResult`). Only the internal status filter changes.

### Changed files

1. **`src/core/tracker.ts`** — In the `next()` method, change the status filter from `["idea", "todo", "in-progress"]` to `["todo"]`
2. **Tests** — Update existing `next` tests and add explicit exclusion tests for `idea` and `in-progress`

## Implementation notes

- This is a single-line change in the filter logic within `Tracker.next()`
- The constant or inline array that defines eligible statuses should be updated
- Search for the existing filter (likely something like `["idea", "todo", "in-progress"].includes(status)`) and narrow to `status === "todo"`
- Update the `NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE` message to say "No todo issues found" instead of "No unblocked issues found" for clarity

## Out of scope

- No changes to the `list` command (it has its own `--status` filter)
- No changes to any other command
- No new CLI flags or options

## Status: DRAFT

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the spec and the current codebase. Let me analyze the spec from a testability and quality perspective.

Key observations:

1. **Current behavior**: The `next()` method filters to `["idea", "todo", "in-progress"]` statuses (line 546)
2. **Proposed change**: Narrow to `["todo"]` only
3. **Current tests**: There are 15 tests in `tracker-next.test.ts`

Let me analyze the spec:

### Testability Concerns

1. **Test "only includes idea, todo, and in-progress statuses" (line 181)**: This test explicitly tests the current behavior that `idea`, `todo`, and `in-progress` are all eligible. After the change, this test would need to be rewritten — the test title and assertions would both be wrong. The spec mentions this in requirement 3, but the test file isn't listed in the "Changed files" section.

2. **Test "returns full ComputedIssue with all expected fields" (line 211)**: At line 230, it asserts `result.status).toBe("idea")` — because `create()` defaults to `idea` status. After the change, this issue won't be returned at all since it's not `todo`. This test will fail. The spec doesn't mention this.

3. **Test "returns the highest-priority unblocked issue for a user" (line 32)**: Creates issues without specifying status — they default to `idea`. After the change, none would be eligible. This test will fail.

4. **Test "excludes issues with active blockages" (line 50)**: Creates issues without specifying status — defaults to `idea`. After the change, none would be eligible and it would return NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE instead of the expected behavior. This test will fail.

5. **Test "uses impact score as tiebreaker" (line 69)**: Same problem — defaults to `idea` status.

6. **Test "returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE when all matching issues are blocked" (line 100)**: Same problem.

7. **Test "includes issues with only resolved blockages" (line 149)**: Same problem.

8. **Test "uses id ASC as final tiebreaker" (line 167)**: Same problem.

9. **Test "does not consider issues assigned to other users" (line 245)**: Same problem.

Basically, **almost every existing test will break** because they create issues with `create()` which defaults to `idea` status. After the change, `idea` status issues won't be considered by `next()`.

### Missing Acceptance Criteria

1. The spec says "All other behavior remains unchanged" but doesn't specify acceptance criteria for the changed behavior. There should be explicit acceptance tests like:
   - Given: user has 1 todo + 1 idea + 1 in-progress issue → next() returns the todo
   - Given: user has only idea issues → next() returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE
   - Given: user has only in-progress issues → next() returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE
   - Given: user has blocked todo + unblocked idea → next() returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE (not the idea)

2. No mention of what happens with the `NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE` message. The spec mentions changing the message to "No todo issues found" in implementation notes — this should be a requirement, not an implementation note.

### Documentation Requirements

1. The JSDoc for `next()` method (tracker.ts:518-524) should be updated to clarify it only considers `todo` status issues.
2. The existing test title "only includes idea, todo, and in-progress statuses" is a documentation of the current behavior — needs updating.
3. If there's user-facing documentation about the `next` command behavior, it needs updating.

### Edge Cases

1. **Default status is `idea`**: The `create()` method creates issues with `idea` status by default. After this change, newly created issues won't show up in `next()` until they're moved to `todo`. This is a significant behavioral change that should be documented clearly.

2. **No `todo` issues at all**: User has only `idea` or `in-progress` work. The `NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE` response is correct, but the message should be specific.

3. **Mixed statuses with blockages**: A blocked `todo` issue should still be excluded, and an unblocked `idea` issue should NOT be a fallback.

4. **Transition period**: If a workflow relies on `next` to show `in-progress` issues as reminders, this change would break that pattern. Not a bug, but worth noting.

5. **CLI tests**: The CLI commands test file (commands.test.ts) also has `next` tests at line 887 — those will also need updating.

# Library Quality Expertise Index
# Agent: library-quality
# Domain: Testing, code quality verification, and documentation for the library

hierarchy:
  manager: cto
  subordinates: [none — leaf agent]

expertise_status: "Active — Phases 1-7 in progress. 569 tests, 1997 assertions, 98.46% tracker.ts line coverage. Prettier + ESLint tooling. Tests refactored into focused per-feature files. Phase 7: `next <user-name>` command validated (20 tests)."

project_phases:
  phase_1_foundation:
    status: complete
    modules: [id, file-io, resolution, index-manager, tracker (init/create/list/view/update/history)]
  phase_2_event_system:
    status: complete
    modules: [events (appendEvent, replayEvents, computeState), types/event.ts]
    description: "Event-sourced issue model — creation, update, comment, comment-update, comment-delete, blockage-added, blockage-resolved, blockage-deleted"
  phase_3_auth_users:
    status: complete
    modules: [auth (resolveAuthor), types/user.ts, types/config.ts, CLI users commands]
    description: "Three auth modes (open/read-only/strict), user registration with tokens, CLI users register/list/revoke/regenerate"
  phase_4_comments:
    status: complete
    modules: [events (computeComments), tracker (commentsAdd/commentsUpdate/commentsDelete/commentsList), cli/commands/comments.ts, cli/runner.ts]
    description: "Comment CRUD — add, update, delete, list comments on issues. computeComments() replays events to build comment state. CLI comments subcommands."
  phase_5_hierarchy:
    status: complete
    modules: [hierarchy.ts (106 lines — isStatusAfter, validateNewChild, validateParentStatusChange, validateReparent, canCloseIssue), index-manager.ts (202 lines — parent/children support), tracker.ts (1025 lines — --parentId on create/update, cascade-close), cli/commands/create.ts, cli/commands/update.ts]
    description: "Issue hierarchy — parent-child relationships via parentId, status progression constraints (child must not be ahead of parent), cascade-close on parent close, reparenting, done parents allowed. CLI --parentId flag on create and update."
  phase_6_blockages:
    status: complete
    modules: [dependency-manager.ts (181 lines — readDependencies, writeDependencies, addBlockage, resolveBlockage, deleteBlockage, getImpactScore, detectCycle), tracker.ts (1329 lines — blockagesAdd/blockagesResolve/blockagesDelete/blockagesList + auto-resolution in update + impact score in list), cli/commands/blockages.ts (107 lines — add/resolve/delete/list actions), cli/runner.ts (164 lines — blockages subcommand registration)]
    description: "Blockage & dependency system — bidirectional dependency maps (blockedBy/blocks), cycle detection (BFS walk), batch atomicity, auto-resolution on done/closed, impact score for list sorting, CLI blockages subcommands (add/resolve/delete/list)."
  phase_7_cli_refinement:
    status: in-progress
    modules: [tracker.ts (1734 lines — next method), cli/commands/next.ts (22 lines), cli/runner.ts (171 lines — next subcommand registration)]
    description: "`next <user-name>` command — suggests the best next issue for a user. Filters to open statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), excludes actively blocked issues, sorts by priority ASC → impact score DESC → id ASC. Returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE when no match. Case-sensitive assignee matching."
    validation:
      total_new_tests: 20
      unit_tests: 14
      cli_tests: 6
      test_scenarios:
        - "Basic case: returns highest-priority unblocked issue for user"
        - "Blocked exclusion: issues with active blockages skipped, next best returned"
        - "Impact tiebreak: when priorities equal, issue blocking others wins"
        - "NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE: returned when no open issues match user or all are blocked"
        - "Done/closed exclusion: only idea/todo/in-progress statuses eligible"
        - "Resolved blockages: issues with only resolved blockages are treated as unblocked"
        - "Case-sensitive matching: 'alice' and 'Alice' match different issues"
        - "Full ComputedIssue: returns all fields (id, title, description, assignee, priority, tags, status, timestamps)"
        - "NOT_INITIALIZED: throws when no .trackgentic/ directory exists"
        - "Cross-user isolation: only considers issues assigned to the specified user"
        - "ID tiebreak: when priority and impact equal, lower id (older) wins"

source_layout:
  total_lines: 3605
  types: [api.ts (134 lines), event.ts (107), index.ts (61), issue.ts (49), index-file.ts (30), user.ts (23), dependency.ts (28), config.ts (9)]
  core: [tracker.ts (1734), dependency-manager.ts (181), index-manager.ts (202), hierarchy.ts (106), events.ts (155), auth.ts (69), file-io.ts (37), errors.ts (31), resolution.ts (27), id.ts (18)]
  cli: [runner.ts (171), commands/blockages.ts (107), commands/comments.ts (102), commands/users.ts (97), commands/update.ts (63), commands/create.ts (51), commands/list.ts (43), commands/next.ts (22), commands/view.ts (22), commands/history.ts (22), commands/init.ts (24), output.ts (13)]
  public_api: "src/index.ts (62 lines) re-exports Tracker class, TrackgenticError, all type and response type aliases"

    examples:

    rule: "Split when a single test file exceeds ~500 lines or covers multiple distinct feature domains"

# Library Quality Expertise Index
# Agent: library-quality
# Domain: Testing, code quality verification, and documentation for the library

hierarchy:
  manager: cto
  subordinates: [none — leaf agent]

expertise_status: "Active — Phases 1-7 in progress. 569 tests, 1997 assertions, 98.46% tracker.ts line coverage. Prettier + ESLint tooling. Tests refactored into focused per-feature files. Phase 7: `next <user-name>` command validated (20 tests)."

project_phases:
  phase_1_foundation:
    status: complete
    modules: [id, file-io, resolution, index-manager, tracker (init/create/list/view/update/history)]
  phase_2_event_system:
    status: complete
    modules: [events (appendEvent, replayEvents, computeState), types/event.ts]
    description: "Event-sourced issue model — creation, update, comment, comment-update, comment-delete, blockage-added, blockage-resolved, blockage-deleted"
  phase_3_auth_users:
    status: complete
    modules: [auth (resolveAuthor), types/user.ts, types/config.ts, CLI users commands]
    description: "Three auth modes (open/read-only/strict), user registration with tokens, CLI users register/list/revoke/regenerate"
  phase_4_comments:
    status: complete
    modules: [events (computeComments), tracker (commentsAdd/commentsUpdate/commentsDelete/commentsList), cli/commands/comments.ts, cli/runner.ts]
    description: "Comment CRUD — add, update, delete, list comments on issues. computeComments() replays events to build comment state. CLI comments subcommands."
  phase_5_hierarchy:
    status: complete
    modules: [hierarchy.ts (106 lines — isStatusAfter, validateNewChild, validateParentStatusChange, validateReparent, canCloseIssue), index-manager.ts (202 lines — parent/children support), tracker.ts (1025 lines — --parentId on create/update, cascade-close), cli/commands/create.ts, cli/commands/update.ts]
    description: "Issue hierarchy — parent-child relationships via parentId, status progression constraints (child must not be ahead of parent), cascade-close on parent close, reparenting, done parents allowed. CLI --parentId flag on create and update."
  phase_6_blockages:
    status: complete
    modules: [dependency-manager.ts (181 lines — readDependencies, writeDependencies, addBlockage, resolveBlockage, deleteBlockage, getImpactScore, detectCycle), tracker.ts (1329 lines — blockagesAdd/blockagesResolve/blockagesDelete/blockagesList + auto-resolution in update + impact score in list), cli/commands/blockages.ts (107 lines — add/resolve/delete/list actions), cli/runner.ts (164 lines — blockages subcommand registration)]
    description: "Blockage & dependency system — bidirectional dependency maps (blockedBy/blocks), cycle detection (BFS walk), batch atomicity, auto-resolution on done/closed, impact score for list sorting, CLI blockages subcommands (add/resolve/delete/list)."
  phase_7_cli_refinement:
    status: in-progress
    modules: [tracker.ts (1734 lines — next method), cli/commands/next.ts (22 lines), cli/runner.ts (171 lines — next subcommand registration)]
    description: "`next <user-name>` command — suggests the best next issue for a user. Filters to open statuses (idea/todo/in-progress), excludes actively blocked issues, sorts by priority ASC → impact score DESC → id ASC. Returns NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE when no match. Case-sensitive assignee matching."
    validation:
      total_new_tests: 20
      unit_tests: 14
      cli_tests: 6
      test_scenarios:
        - "Basic case: returns highest-priority unblocked issue for user"
        - "Blocked exclusion: issues with active blockages skipped, next best returned"
        - "Impact tiebreak: when priorities equal, issue blocking others wins"
        - "NO_ISSUES_AVAILABLE: returned when no open issues match user or all are blocked"
        - "Done/closed exclusion: only idea/todo/in-progress statuses eligible"
        - "Resolved blockages: issues with only resolved blockages are treated as unblocked"
        - "Case-sensitive matching: 'alice' and 'Alice' match different issues"
        - "Full ComputedIssue: returns all fields (id, title, description, assignee, priority, tags, status, timestamps)"
        - "NOT_INITIALIZED: throws when no .trackgentic/ directory exists"
        - "Cross-user isolation: only considers issues assigned to the specified user"
        - "ID tiebreak: when priority and impact equal, lower id (older) wins"

  core: [tracker.ts (1734), dependency-manager.ts (181), index-manager.ts (202), hierarchy.ts (106), events.ts (155), auth.ts (69), file-io.ts (37), errors.ts (31), resolution.ts (27), id.ts (18)]

    examples:

    rule: "Split when a single test file exceeds ~500 lines or covers multiple distinct feature domains"

  split_organization:

        tracker-comments: { tests: 26, lines: 454, describes: ["comments (add/update/delete/list + error paths)"] }
        tracker-hierarchy: { tests: 23, lines: 550, describes: ["hierarchy — create with parentId", "hierarchy — update status with children (downward)", "hierarchy — update status upward promotion", "hierarchy — reparenting"] }
        tracker-blockages: { tests: 30, lines: 623, describes: ["blockages", "blockages auth integration"] }
        tracker-auth: { tests: 19, lines: 364, describes: ["auth integration", "comments auth integration", "auth + hierarchy integration"] }
        tracker-users: { tests: 14, lines: 206, describes: ["user management (usersRegister, usersList, usersRevoke, usersRegenerate)"] }
        tracker-next: { tests: 14, lines: 256, describes: ["next() — suggest best issue for user"] }
    events_split:
      monolithic: "events.test.ts (543 lines, 23 tests)"
      split_total: "4 files, 575 lines, 23 tests — same test count, organized by function"
      files:
        append-event: { tests: 3, lines: 88, describes: ["appendEvent"] }

        compute-comments: { tests: 8, lines: 212, describes: ["computeComments"] }
        replay-events: { tests: 2, lines: 54, describes: ["replayEvents"] }

  other_test_files:

    core_dependency_manager: { tests: 19, lines: 206, describes: [addBlockage, resolveBlockage, deleteBlockage, getImpactScore, detectCycle] }
    core_index_manager: { tests: ~32, lines: 366, describes: [insertEntry, findEntry, updateEntry, removeEntry, parent/children index operations] }

tooling:
  formatter: prettier (v3.5.3)
  prettier_config: { semi: true, singleQuote: false, tabWidth: 2, trailingComma: "all", printWidth: 100 }
  linter: eslint (v9.28.0) with typescript-eslint (v8.33.0)
  eslint_highlights:
    - "Flat config (eslint.config.js), not legacy .eslintrc"
    - "no-explicit-any set to warn, not error"
    - "Unused vars with _ prefix ignored (argsIgnorePattern + varsIgnorePattern)"
  test_runner: bun test (v1.3.11)
  coverage: "bun test --coverage with coverageThreshold=0 in bunfig.toml (report only, no per-file gate)"
  typechecker: "tsc --noEmit with strict + 4 extra flags (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitOverride, noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature)"
  docs: "typedoc v0.28 with typedoc-plugin-markdown, validated via typedoc --emit none"
  quality_gate: "bun run quality = typecheck + lint + test:coverage (all must pass)"

learnings:
  biome:  # DEPRECATED — Biome removed, Prettier + ESLint in use
    - "Biome was replaced by Prettier + ESLint in Phase 3. These learnings are historical only."
  eslint_prettier:
    - "ESLint flat config uses `import.meta.dirname` for tsconfigRootDir."
    - "typescript-eslint recommended config + eslint recommended is the base."
    - "Separate rules blocks for src/ and tests/ — tests don't use projectService."
  bun_coverage:
    - "Bun's coverageThreshold in bunfig.toml applies per-file, not just overall."
    - "coverageThreshold=0 means report coverage without failing on per-file thresholds."
    - "Current coverage: 98.46% line on tracker.ts (all other core at 100%), 100% function across all source files."
  typescript:
    - "4 extra strict flags (noUncheckedIndexedAccess, exactOptionalPropertyTypes, noImplicitOverride, noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature) are enabled and pass clean."
    - "noPropertyAccessFromIndexSignature requires bracket notation for dynamic keys (e.g. process.env['TRACKGENTIC_USER_TOKEN'])."
    - "exactOptionalPropertyTypes means `undefined` cannot be assigned to optional properties explicitly."
  typedoc:
    - "TypeDoc with typedoc-plugin-markdown parses all source files without errors."
    - "The --emit none flag is useful for CI validation without generating files."
    - "Config: excludePrivate=true, excludeInternal=true, categorizeByGroup=true, categoryOrder=[Core, Types, Errors, *]"
  testing_patterns:
    - "Test helpers: createTestDir/cleanupTestDir for fs isolation, createIssueParams/updateIssueParams factory functions, expectError/expectOk assertions."
    - "Tracker tests use real filesystem (tmpdir) — no mocks. This gives integration-level confidence."
    - "Each test file uses beforeEach to create a fresh test directory and afterEach to clean up."
    - "CLI command tests spawn the CLI via bun subprocess and check stdout/exit codes."
    - "auth.test.ts tests resolveAuthor in isolation — doesn't need Tracker instance."
    - "Split test files: tracker/ and events/ subdirectories contain feature-scoped slices. Each file focuses on one domain (CRUD, auth, comments, hierarchy, blockages, users, init). All split files nest under a top-level describe('Tracker') or describe('Event Engine') to maintain discoverability."
    - "Monolithic files tracker.test.ts and events.test.ts still exist but are deprecated — the split files in tracker/ and events/ contain the same tests reorganized."
    - "File naming: tracker-{feature}.test.ts for Tracker slices, {function-name}.test.ts for event engine slices."
    - "Split threshold: ~500 lines or multiple distinct feature domains triggers a split."
    - "resolution.ts has 93.75% coverage — the filesystem-root walk-up edge case (parent === current) is untested."
    - "Hierarchy tests: pure validation functions in hierarchy.test.ts, integration tests in tracker/tracker-hierarchy.test.ts (cascade-close, reparent, status constraints)."
    - "Auth integration tests spread across: tracker/tracker-auth.test.ts (general + comments + hierarchy auth), tracker/tracker-blockages.test.ts (blockages auth)."
    - "Edge cases tested: same status no-op on update, reparent from null, same parentId no-op, done parent allowed, mixed cascade (multiple children at different statuses), already-closed children."
  error_handling:
    - "TrackgenticError carries result code, message, and exitCode. 13 error codes defined."
    - "ErrorCodes: NOT_INITIALIZED(1), ALREADY_INITIALIZED(0), TOKEN_REQUIRED(2), INVALID_TOKEN(3), DEFAULT_USER_MISSING(4), NOT_FOUND(5), ISSUE_MISSING(6), COMMENT_NOT_FOUND(7), USER_ALREADY_EXISTS(8), USER_NOT_FOUND(9), INVALID_PARAMS(10), BLOCKAGE_CYCLE(11), HIERARCHY_CONSTRAINT(12)."
    - "HIERARCHY_CONSTRAINT (exit 12) used in Phase 5 — parent status would constrain child, or parent is closed."
    - "BLOCKAGE_CYCLE (exit 11) now active — used in Phase 6 blockages when addBlockage would create a cycle."
  phase_5_hierarchy:
    - "hierarchy.ts exports pure validation functions: isStatusAfter, validateNewChild, validateParentStatusChange, validateReparent, canCloseIssue."
    - "index-manager.ts extended to track parentId and children arrays in IndexEntry."
    - "Cascade-close: when parent transitions to closed, all non-closed children are auto-closed recursively."
    - "Status progression: idea -> todo -> in-progress -> done -> closed. Child must not be ahead of parent."
    - "Done parents ARE allowed — the constraint is only on closed parents for new children."
    - "CLI --parentId flag added to create and update commands."
    - "Prettier formatting issues found and fixed in test files during quality validation."
  phase_6_blockages:
    - "dependency-manager.ts is a pure function module — all functions take DependenciesFile and return a new DependenciesFile (immutable pattern). No filesystem I/O inside pure functions; readDependencies/writeDependencies are separate async functions."
    - "Bidirectional maps (blockedBy + blocks) must always stay in sync. Every mutation function (add/resolve/delete) updates both maps. Tests verify both sides explicitly."
    - "detectCycle uses BFS walk of blockedBy graph from blockerId. Only considers active entries. Uses visited set for safety against pre-existing cycles."
    - "Batch atomicity: blockagesAdd validates ALL blockers against a projected (deep-cloned) state before writing. If any causes a cycle, the entire batch is rejected with no side effects."
    - "Auto-resolution: integrated into Tracker.update() AFTER main update logic. When issue transitions to done/closed, reads deps, filters active blocks entries, resolves each, appends system-authored blockage-resolved events to blocked issues."
    - "Impact score in list sort: readDependenciesSync (synchronous) used inside sort comparator because sort is synchronous. Lazy-loads deps once during sort."
    - "Impact score sort order: priority ASC → impact score DESC → id ASC (age). Higher impact (more issues blocked) sorts first within same priority."
